steps.title: Identify the claim
steps.content: The claim is that Aristophanes satirizes sophists as having an exaggerated sense of their own wisdom. The supporting quotation must show a sophist boasting about his own cleverness.

steps.title: Match the quotation
steps.content:
Choice C has Socrates, identified as a sophist, refer to 'any clever dogma about abstruse matters' he propounds — a self-aggrandizing description of his own teachings.

  • A talks about Strepsiades praising sophists
  • B describes the business in general
  • D criticizes a customer
; only C shows a sophist puffing up his own wisdom.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
